briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the Climate Briefing for March 5, 2026. Today, as severe ecosystem threats and regional economic difficulties due to climate change are reported globally, various efforts are continuing both domestically and internationally to achieve carbon neutrality and transition to renewable energy.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Ecosystem Disruption Warnings
sections.content: Recently, human-induced climate change has led to early spring heatwaves in the central United States, increasing the risk of severe weather [46], and climate change is melting permafrost, raising landslide risks [51]. Severe impacts across ecosystems are being observed, including the disruption of tropical plant flowering seasons and most tropical insects reaching their thermal limits [53, 55]. Furthermore, concerns about biodiversity loss are growing, with reports of climate change exacerbating tropical diseases, altering the chemical composition of rainwater [57, 64], and even reducing bird sounds in seemingly healthy rainforests [65]. In particular, rising temperatures are threatening fisheries in Greenland [67], and the deepening forest disturbance in Europe and difficulties in apple cultivation due to warm winters demonstrate the direct economic impact of climate change on regional economies [72, 73].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges in Climate Policy and Economic Issues
sections.content: In the U.S., the Florida House of Representatives voted to halt local governments' 'net-zero policies' aimed at climate change mitigation, signaling a policy rollback [54]. Domestically, a machinery manufacturer was fined 257.6 billion KRW for falsely announcing plans to enter the eco-friendly energy business, engaging in stock manipulation and tax evasion, highlighting the severity of economic crimes that undermine trust in climate crisis response efforts [45]. Additionally, Incheon still relies 95% on fossil fuels, making the transition to renewable energy an urgent task [35]. Meanwhile, internationally, it was reported that the primary reason Reese's changed its chocolate products was climate change, not corporate greed, demonstrating the direct impact of climate change on corporate operations and consumer products [61].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Active movements to achieve carbon neutrality are spreading across various regions in Korea. Jeonwon Social Welfare Corporation and Senior Club in Yangsan City declared carbon neutrality, resolving to transition senior job sites to eco-friendly practices [11, 17]. In Dongdaemun-gu, the '4th Youth Climate Volunteer Group' was launched, practicing carbon neutrality with young people [12, 28]. The Chungbuk Office of Education has designated all schools as carbon-neutral schools, intensifying environmental education [15, 22]. Gwangmyeong City is recruiting 10 citizen-led '1.5℃ Climate Volunteer' clubs with the goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 [9, 16, 29]. Cheonan City is fostering community-wide participation by establishing a cooperative system with universities and support centers and launching joint educational programs to create a carbon-neutral city by its citizens [18, 21, 24, 30]. Furthermore, Gunsan City and Seogwipo City are promoting a car carbon neutrality point system to encourage citizens' eco-friendly driving [25, 26, 31], and Samcheok City has strengthened its foundation as a carbon-neutral hydrogen city by supplying 57 hydrogen electric vehicles [32].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Transition to Renewable Energy
sections.content: Specific efforts for renewable energy transition are continuing domestically. Incheon City is re-challenging for 'Distributed Energy Special Zone' amidst the trend of non-capital region renewable energy hubs [33, 34]. Taean-gun is benchmarking Singapore to strengthen its growth foundation by attracting renewable energy investments [36]. The Jeonnam Headquarters of Korea Rural Community Corporation has initiated steps to expand the designation of renewable energy zones [37], and the Sudokwon Air Quality Management Office held public-private meetings to explore cooperation for renewable energy dissemination in the Seoul Metropolitan Area [38, 39]. Notably, a study found that installing solar power facilities on the roofs of industrial complexes nationwide has the potential to produce electricity equivalent to three nuclear power plants [43], demonstrating significant potential for large-scale renewable energy expansion. Concurrently, Kia's purpose-built vehicle (PBV), 'The Kia PV5' cargo model, became the first electric vehicle to rank first in sales in the domestic commercial vehicle market, signaling a green light for the transition to eco-friendly mobility [42].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response Efforts and Cooperation
sections.content: The international community is making various efforts to address climate change. Tokyo, Japan, as a carbon-neutral experimental city, plans to fully launch its hydrogen city project in 2026 [27]. The UK's greenhouse gas emissions decreased by 2.4% in 2025, with coal use reaching its lowest level in 400 years, showing positive results [76]. Google is fulfilling its corporate social responsibility by pledging approximately three hours of its annual revenue to combat climate change [60], and the Environmental Committee approved the Climate Change Superfund bill, raising expectations for climate finance [56]. Meanwhile, the European Union (EU) is accelerating the eco-friendly transition across industries by unveiling its 'Made in Europe' strategy and proposing plans to increase low-carbon steel production [44, 77]. International solidarity for climate justice is also strengthening, with Louisiana joining climate change litigation [59] and Vanuatu pushing for a UN climate resolution despite opposition from the Trump administration [75]. China also expressed its commitment to climate action by unveiling new five-year climate targets at the 'Two Sessions' meeting [78].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, the impacts of the climate crisis are intensifying globally, but policy, technological, and civil society efforts, both domestic and international, to overcome it are also actively underway. We must continue to show interest and take action to build a better future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: Last month, Kia's first Purpose-Built Vehicle (PBV), 'The Kia PV5' cargo model, achieved the top sales position in the domestic commercial vehicle market, marking a first among electric vehicles.
summary.cause: Analysis indicates that higher subsidies compared to conventional passenger cars and the vehicle's versatility, allowing it to be used for various customer-desired purposes, were crucial factors in achieving the top sales position.

articles.title: [Exclusive] Utilizing Industrial Complex Roofs Nationwide Could Yield Power Equivalent to 3 Nuclear Reactors
articles.original_title: [단독] 전국 산업단지 지붕만 잘 활용해도 원전 3기 효과
articles.description: Solar power generation facilities are installed on the roofs of companies in the Golden Root Industrial Complex in Gimhae, Gyeongnam. (Provided by Gimhae City) A study has revealed that installing solar power facilities on the roofs of industrial complexes nationwide could generate electricity equivalent to three nuclear power plants. According to data on the potential for renewable energy installation on large idle sites, obtained by Rep. Park Ji-hye's office (Democratic Party of Korea) from the Ministry of Climate, Energy and Environment, the government plans to install solar power facilities on industrial complex roofs nationwide...

summary.phenomenon: According to data obtained by Rep. Park Ji-hye's office (Democratic Party of Korea) from the Ministry of Climate, Energy and Environment, a study indicates that installing solar power generation facilities on the roofs of industrial complexes nationwide has the potential to produce electricity equivalent to three nuclear power plants.
summary.outlook: The utilization of solar power generation facilities on the roofs of industrial complexes nationwide is expected to produce electricity equivalent to three nuclear power plants, thereby contributing to the domestic power supply.

articles.title: EU Unveils 'Made in Europe' Strategy Targeting China
articles.original_title: EU, 중국 겨냥 ‘메이드 인 유럽’ 전략 공개
articles.description: In response to the influx of low-cost Chinese imports, the EU has announced a draft 'Industrial Acceleration Act (IAA)' and unveiled its 'Made in Europe' strategy. This strategy includes 'EU-origin' requirements for public procurement and subsidies. While products from countries with EU FTAs are to be included as 'EU-origin,' concerns remain for Korean electric vehicles regarding subsidies, necessitating countermeasures. The European Union (EU) has revealed its 'Made in Europe' strategy to counter low-cost imports, including Chinese electric vehicles. Although products from countries with free trade agreements (FTAs) with the EU are to be considered 'EU-origin,' there are still concerns about 'intra-regional differences' regarding EV subsidies, suggesting a need for countermeasures.

summary.phenomenon: The European Union (EU) has unveiled its 'Made in Europe' strategy and announced a draft Industrial Acceleration Act (IAA) in response to the influx of low-cost imports, including Chinese electric vehicles.
summary.cause: This initiative stems from the recognition of the need to develop countermeasures against the offensive of low-cost Chinese imports.
summary.outlook: The 'EU-origin' requirement is expected to be applied to public procurement and subsidies, which could pose a burden on Korean electric vehicles from countries with EU FTAs, thus necessitating the development of countermeasures.

articles.original_description: 중국산 저가 수입품 공세에 대응 산업가속화법 ‘IAA’ 초안 발표 공공 조달·보조금에 ‘EU산’ 요건 한국 전기차도 부담, 대응책 필요유럽연합(EU)이 중국산 전기차를 포함한 저가 수입품에 대응하기 위한 ‘메이드 인 유럽’ 전략을 공개했다. EU와 자유무역협정(FTA)을 맺은 국가 제품은 ‘EU산’에 포함하기로 했지만, 전기차 보조금 등에 대해선 ‘역내 차···

articles.title: 257.6 Billion Won Surcharge Imposed for Stock Manipulation and Tax Evasion
articles.original_title: 주가조작 탈루에 2576억 추징금
articles.description: The National Tax Service investigated 27 entities for false disclosures, low-price asset transfers to children, and backdoor listings. Company A, a machinery manufacturer, falsely announced plans to enter the eco-friendly energy business, invested 10 billion won in a shell company, and then siphoned off nearly 3 billion won in investment funds to its owner. When the new business initiative was revealed to be false, the stock price plummeted by two-thirds and the company was delisted, causing significant losses for general shareholders. The owner...

summary.phenomenon: According to a National Tax Service investigation, machinery manufacturer Company A was assessed a surcharge of 257.6 billion won for stock manipulation and tax evasion, including falsely announcing plans to enter the eco-friendly energy business and siphoning off investment funds to its owner through a shell company.
summary.cause: This was due to Company A manipulating its stock price by falsely disclosing plans to enter a new eco-friendly energy business and siphoning off investment funds using a shell company.
summary.outlook: Due to Company A's false disclosures, its stock price plummeted and the company was delisted, resulting in significant losses for general shareholders. The National Tax Service is expected to continue investigations into similar illegal activities.

articles.original_description: 국세청, 허위공시 등 27곳 조사…자녀에 헐값 증여·우회 상장도 적발기계장치 제조업체인 A사는 친환경 에너지 관련 사업 진출 계획을 거짓으로 공시하고, 페이퍼 컴퍼니에 100억원을 출자한 뒤 사주에게 30억원 가까운 투자금을 빼돌렸다. 그러나 신사업 추진이 거짓으로 밝혀지면서 주가는 3분의 1 토막으로 폭락하고 상장폐지돼 일반 주주들은 큰 손실을 봤다.사주···
